div.h2: Min-Heap Timer Structure: Array Representation and Parent-Child Relationships

div.p: Heap Invariant (must hold at all times): ∀ i > 0 : entries[ (i−1)/2 ].expiry_ms ≤ entries[i].expiry_ms
Equivalently: every parent expires before or at the same time as both its children. Consequence: entries[0] (the root) is always the nearest-expiring timer.
compute_epoll_timeout() reads entries[0].expiry_ms in O(1) — no scan required.
Violated by: inserting without sift_up · removing without sift_down · heap_swap without updating heap_idx and conn_t.timer_id
struct timer_entry_t { uint64_t expiry_ms; int fd; int heap_idx; } sizeof = 16 bytes per entry · 7 entries = 112 bytes total
Back-pointer contract: conn_table[fd].timer_id == heap_idx heap.entries[heap_idx].fd == fd
Maintained by every heap_swap(): entries[a].heap_idx = a entries[b].heap_idx = b conn_table[entries[a].fd].timer_id = a conn_table[entries[b].fd].timer_id = b
Enables O(log n) cancel: timer_cancel(conn->timer_id) → no linear scan needed
